name: Diesel (Safe, Extensible Rust ORM)

auth.notes: ORM library; DB connection credentials managed by application

performance.notes: Zero-cost abstractions; query built at compile time; near raw-SQL performance

agent_readiness.notes: Type-safe Rust ORM and query builder. Schema defined in schema.rs (generated by diesel print-schema). #[derive(Queryable, Insertable, Selectable)]. dsl::users.filter(name.eq('Alice')).load::<User>(&conn). Migrations via diesel migration. Compile-time query type checking (invalid queries = compile errors). Postgres, MySQL, SQLite backends. Synchronous by default (diesel-async for async). No runtime schema inference. Steep learning curve but maximum safety. Used in production Rust web apps.

gotchas: No async support in core (use diesel-async separately)
Steep learning curve — compile errors from type system
schema.rs must be regenerated after migrations
best_when: Rust production apps needing compile-time SQL correctness
Projects where SQL errors should be caught at compile time
Teams who accept the learning curve for maximum type safety
avoid_when: Prototyping (sqlx or SeaORM faster to start)
Dynamic queries (Diesel's type system can fight you)
summary: Diesel is a type-safe, synchronous Rust ORM and query builder. Compile-time SQL validation via Rust's type system — invalid queries won't compile. #[derive(Queryable, Insertable)] macros. Schema from diesel print-schema. Postgres/MySQL/SQLite. diesel-async for async support. Maximum safety with steep learning curve. sqlx for simpler async SQL; SeaORM for async-first ORM.
